title: Boat Rental in Sardinia: A 2026 Cost and Selection Guide
html:
Choosing a boat to rent in Sardinia depends on your group size, budget, and the waters you want to explore. Depart from Porto Cervo or Olbia for the Costa Smeralda, with prices starting from EUR 250 for a half-day motorboat on BoatHire24, the worldwide marketplace where you can compare verified boat listings.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Sardinia?

Prices range from EUR 200 for a shared tour to EUR 2,500 for a crewed luxury yacht per day. The cost varies by boat type, season, and duration. Below is a typical price breakdown:

  • Shared boat tour: from EUR 80 per person (half-day, up to 12 guests)
  • Small motorboat (5, 6 m): EUR 250, 400 half-day, up to 6 people
  • Catamaran full-day (12, 15 m): EUR 800, 1,400, up to 10 guests
  • Crewed yacht (15+ m, skipper included): EUR 1,500, 2,500 per day

Where to set off in Sardinia

Sardinia offers countless departure points, but the most convenient are concentrated along the north and northeast coasts. Marinella di Porto Cervo and Marina di Portisco provide excellent facilities for motorboats and luxury yachts. Further south, Marina di Villasimius and Cagliari Harbour offer access to the Golfo degli Angeli. For the Maddalena Archipelago, set off from Palau or Cannigione. The Bay of Orosei is best reached from Santa Maria Navarrese. Each location has its own charm and proximity to anchorages.

Which boat to rent in Sardinia: a step-by-step choice

Your choice depends on group size, desired speed, and whether you want a skipper. Consider these popular options:

  1. Motorboat (5, 7 m): Best for small groups of 4, 6 exploring coves; from EUR 250 half-day.
  2. Rigid inflatable (RIB): Ideal for speed and reaching the Maddalena Archipelago in 30 minutes; seats 6, 8, from EUR 350 half-day.
  3. Sailboat (9, 11 m): Perfect for a relaxing day sail, capacity 6, 8; from EUR 450 full-day.
  4. Catamaran (12, 15 m): Stable and spacious for up to 10; from EUR 800 full-day.
  5. Luxury motor yacht (15+ m): Crewed, for 8, 12 guests; from EUR 1,500 full-day. Check this speedboat listing for a popular day-charter example.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Sardinia?

For most small motorboats (up to 40 hp or under 10 m), a licence is not required, but the operator will confirm exact requirements. In Italy, a patente nautica is mandatory for boats over 40 hp or longer than 10 m. However, many rental companies include a skipper or offer licence-free boats if you stick to the power and size limits. Always check the listing details: each boat on BoatHire24 states licence and skipper options.

A useful tip: book a boat with a skipper for your first day to learn the local navigation rules and discover hidden beaches such as Cala Granu.

When is the best season to rent a boat in Sardinia?

The peak season runs from June to September, with July and August being the busiest and most expensive. The Mistral wind can affect the western coast, while the Levante is milder. For calmer seas and lower prices, consider May or September. Water temperatures reach 24°C in August. In 2026, expect strong demand for August; book early.

At a glance

  • Price range: from EUR 200 (shared tour) to EUR 2,500 per day (crewed yacht)
  • Peak months: June, September
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half-day) to 8 hours (full-day)
  • Capacity: 2, 12 guests
  • Currency: EUR
  • Licence: Not always needed; check listing

Questions about boat rental in Sardinia

Can I rent a boat in Sardinia without a licence?

Yes, many small motorboats under 40 hp and under 10 metres are available without a licence, and operators often include a skipper if required.

What is the best area to rent a boat in Sardinia?

The Costa Smeralda and Maddalena Archipelago are the most popular, with sheltered waters and turquoise beaches, but the south offers quieter anchorages.

How far is the Maddalena Archipelago from Porto Cervo?

The distance from Porto Cervo to Palau is about 13 nautical miles, and from Palau to La Maddalena island it is just 5 nautical miles.

Can I rent a boat for a whole week in Sardinia?

Yes, many companies offer weekly charters for catamarans and yachts, often with discounts compared to daily rates.

BoatHire24 makes it easy to find the perfect vessel for your Sardinian adventure, whether you want a self-drive motorboat or a crewed yacht. If you own a boat in Sardinia, you can list a boat for free and reach thousands of daily visitors. Book with confidence knowing each listing is verified.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Sardinia and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
